description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a wooden fuselage and dual fabric-covered wings, supported by a network of struts and wires. The wings are straight and stacked one above the other, typical of early 20th-century aircraft. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers towards the tail, which features a conventional tail assembly with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear consists of two main floats, indicating this is a seaplane designed for water landings. The cockpit is open and situated near the front of the fuselage, likely accommodating a single pilot. The overall color scheme includes natural wood tones, white wings, and dark accents on the wingtips and tail.
description: A vintage wooden biplane seaplane with dual white wings, open cockpit, and twin floats for water landings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the wooden fuselage, stacked white wings, and the float undercarriage.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual wings, open cockpit, and the symmetry of the floats and tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with clear visibility of the tail and wing structure.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail assembly, the alignment of the wings, and the structure of the floats.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, high-mounted wings stacked vertically, supported by struts and wires, no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single piston engine mounted at the nose, with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Float landing gear with two main floats for water landings, fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located near the nose of the fuselage, likely single-seat.
